data.title: Aster: Launches BAY Perpetual Contracts and introduces a $50,000 reward pool.
data.description: Aster announced on its X platform that it will launch BAY perpetual contracts and introduce a $50,000 ASTER reward pool. BAYUSDT perpetual contracts will launch at 12:00 UTC on April 8th, and the related activities will last until 14:00 on April 15th. During the event, BAYUSDT perpetual contracts will receive a 1.2x bonus. Rewards will be distributed based on the user's trading fees in BAYUSDT perpetual contracts, with a maximum of 3% per user. Users must accumulate more than $5 in trading fees to qualify; rewards below 1 ASTER will not be awarded, and fraudulent trading and manipulation will result in disqualification.

data.title: Qatar Resumes Production at LNG Export Facilities
data.description: Qatar has initiated efforts to restart production at its liquefied natural gas (LNG) export facilities. According to Odaily, this move comes as part of the country's strategy to enhance its energy export capabilities. The resumption of operations is expected to bolster Qatar's position in the global LNG market.

data.title: Sky Partners' Rune's two long positions in crude oil have turned from profitable to losing, with the overall position now showing a floating loss of over $610,000.
data.description: With Brent crude oil falling below $90 per barrel, Hyperbot data monitoring shows that Rune, co-founder of Sky (formerly MakerDAO), has seen his 20x leveraged long positions in WTI crude oil futures (CL) and 7x leveraged long positions in Brent crude oil futures turn to losses. In addition, his 7x leveraged short positions in the Nasdaq 100 and 20x leveraged short positions in the S&P 500 are also in a loss-making state. His total position value has now fallen to about $3.952 million, with a floating loss of over $610,000.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Jack Yi: Long-Term Bullish on Crypto but Warns on Volatility and Cycles
data.description: Key TakeawaysJack Yi remains long-term bullish on crypto markets.Comments made during a Binance Square AMA livestream.Emphasizes importance of respecting market cycles and extreme volatility.Experience spans multiple bull and bear cycles since 2015.Veteran Investor Shares Outlook in Binance Square AMAJack Yi, founder of Liquid Capital, reiterated a long-term bullish stance on crypto during a Binance Square AMA livestream.Yi noted that he entered the market in late 2015 and has since navigated multiple bull and bear cycles.Long-Term Bullish Trend Remains IntactAccording to Yi, maintaining a long-term bullish view has historically been the more effective strategy in crypto.Despite repeated downturns, the market has continued to trend upward over time, supported by growing adoption and expanding institutional participation.Warning Against Over-OptimismHowever, Yi cautioned against excessive optimism.He stressed that investors must remain aware of:Cyclical market behaviorSharp drawdownsHigh volatility environmentsIgnoring these factors, he said, can lead to significant losses during market corrections.

data.title: Pharos Secures $44 Million in Series A Funding to Enhance RWA Infrastructure
data.description: Layer 1 network Pharos has announced the completion of a $44 million Series A funding round on April 8. According to BlockBeats, the round was led by several undisclosed major institutions, including an Asian private equity fund, a new energy public company, and a Hong Kong-compliant financial institution. The funds will be used to accelerate the development of on-chain real-world asset (RWA) infrastructure across Asia and globally.

data.title: Crypto Market Rebound Boosts Huang Licheng's Leveraged Positions
data.description:
Hyperbot data reveals that Huang Licheng has opened a 10x leveraged long position in HYPE, currently holding 28,888 HYPE tokens. According to Odaily, he has also incrementally increased his 25x leveraged long position in Ethereum, now holding 10,925 ETH with a liquidation price of $2,066.

As the cryptocurrency market rebounds, Huang Licheng's overall positions have shifted from loss to profit, with a total value reaching approximately $25.651 million and a floating profit of around $1.1 million.

data.title: Qatar begins work to resume production at its liquefied natural gas export plants.
data.description: Market news: Qatar has begun work to resume production at its liquefied natural gas (LNG) export plants. (Jinshi)

data.title: Cryptocurrency Fraud Losses Rise to $11.366 Billion in 2025, FBI Reports
data.description: The FBI has reported a significant increase in cryptocurrency-related fraud losses, with its Internet Crime Complaint Center recording $11.366 billion in losses for 2025. According to NS3.AI, this marks a 22% rise compared to the previous year. Individuals aged 60 and older experienced the highest losses, totaling $4.43 billion. Cryptocurrency investment fraud continues to be the largest scam category, accounting for $7.228 billion of the total losses.

data.title: Iran to Restrict Reopening of Waterways, Coordination with Military Required
data.description: Iranian senior officials have announced that the reopening of waterways will be subject to restrictions. According to Odaily, coordination with the Iranian military will be necessary, and all vessels will still need to obtain permission.

data.title: Ethereum Researchers Explore 'Block-in-Blobs' Proposal to Enhance Scalability
data.description:
Ethereum researchers are investigating the 'Block-in-Blobs' proposal (EIP-8142) to encode block execution load data into blobs. According to ChainCatcher, this approach allows validators to verify data existence through data availability sampling (DAS) without downloading the entire dataset, thereby reducing bandwidth pressure and improving scalability.

In the zkEVM context, the proposal aims to address the gap in data availability assurance that zero-knowledge proofs cannot cover. It also plans to unify execution gas and blob data usage into a single 'data gas' system. Additionally, Biconomy, in collaboration with the Ethereum Foundation, has proposed ERC-8211, which supports executing multiple operations in sequence under a single signature. This aims to reduce failed transactions and optimize the DeFi interaction experience.

data.title: Energy Markets Respond to US-Iran Ceasefire
data.description: Energy markets have shown a reaction to the recent two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran, marking a potential easing of the Middle East energy supply shock. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that while this development is a positive sign, the resolution of the broader energy supply issues will require more time. The ceasefire is seen as a preliminary step towards stabilizing the region's energy dynamics, but the complexities involved suggest that a full recovery will not be immediate.

data.title: U.S. Considers Joint Venture with Iran in Strait of Hormuz
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the United States is contemplating a 'joint venture' with Iran to ensure the security of the Strait of Hormuz. According to NS3.AI, this statement follows the announcement of a two-week ceasefire agreement with Iran on Tuesday. Trump indicated that peace negotiations might commence on Friday, with U.S. troops staying in the region to uphold any forthcoming agreements.

data.title: Gate will launch Checkmate (CHECK) spot and instant trading.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Gate will launch Checkmate (CHECK) spot trading at 22:00 (UTC+8) on April 8, and will also launch Checkmate (CHECK) on the Flash Exchange platform at 23:00 (UTC+8).

data.title: Polymarket Acquires DeFi Infrastructure Firm Brahma
data.description: Polymarket has announced the acquisition of DeFi infrastructure company Brahma. According to Foresight News, Brahma was co-founded by Alessandro Tenconi, Akanshu Jain, and Bapireddy Karri, who will continue to play key roles in infrastructure, protocol design, and product integration. This integration aims to enhance the trading reliability, execution speed, and capital efficiency of the Polymarket platform, while also reducing the entry barriers for new users and improving interoperability across blockchain networks.

data.title: U.S. and Iran to Collaborate on Nuclear Dust Removal, Trump Announces
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the United States will closely cooperate with Iran following a significant regime change in the country. According to ChainCatcher, Trump stated that uranium enrichment activities will be prohibited, and the U.S. will work with Iran to excavate and remove all deeply buried nuclear dust, which was deployed by B-2 bombers.

These areas have been under strict satellite surveillance, managed by the Space Force, and have remained untouched since the attack. Discussions between the U.S. and Iran regarding tariff and sanction relief are ongoing, with many items already agreed upon.

data.title: Potential Dollar Decline Amid Iran Conflict and Trump Administration Risks
data.description:
Analysts from UniCredit Bank, led by Roberto Mialich, suggest that the U.S. dollar could face further declines if the conflict in Iran concludes or becomes a risk factor for the Trump administration. According to Odaily, Mialich highlights that the dollar's role as a safe haven could diminish if risk appetite improves, as observed after U.S. President Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire.

Mialich notes that once the conflict ends, markets may refocus on factors that previously pressured the dollar before the war. Additionally, if the U.S. becomes embroiled in a prolonged conflict without a clear exit strategy, the dollar might weaken further. This situation could lead to renewed concerns about U.S. economic policy and the credibility of the dollar.

data.title: Thai SEC Proposes Regulatory Oversight for Crypto Enterprise Funders
data.description:
The Thai Securities and Exchange Commission (Thai SEC) has proposed extending regulatory oversight to include the funders behind major shareholders of cryptocurrency enterprises. According to ChainCatcher, this proposal aims to ensure that both direct and indirect supporters of these enterprises undergo the same level of regulatory scrutiny as shareholders.

The initiative seeks to curb hidden financial flows and prevent illegal funds from infiltrating crypto businesses. The rules would also apply to those providing financial support through equity acquisitions. The Thai SEC stated that 'significant financial support' includes guarantees, contractual arrangements, or any investment tools that effectively position the funder as an investor. If the primary shareholder is a government-related entity, only the entity-level shareholding will be reviewed.

data.title: World Liberty Financial Launches Ethereum-Based Lending Market
data.description: World Liberty Financial has introduced a new lending market within the Ethereum ecosystem by integrating with the decentralized lending protocol Dolomite. According to ChainCatcher, the current yield for USD1 stands at 34.51%. It is important to note that interest rates are subject to change, and the most accurate rates can be found on the product's website.

data.title: Standard Chartered Considers Internal Management of Zodia Custody
data.description: Standard Chartered is contemplating bringing certain operations of its digital asset storage company, Zodia Custody, under internal management. According to BlockBeats, the bank plans to merge Zodia's crypto custody services with its Luxembourg digital asset division. Meanwhile, Zodia will continue to operate independently as a software-as-a-service business.

data.title: Trump's TACO Move Boosts Markets Amid US-Iran Ceasefire Uncertainty
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's recent TACO initiative has led to a surge in market activity, Bloomberg posted on X. However, the newly established two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran continues to leave significant issues unaddressed. The ceasefire aims to reduce tensions but does not resolve underlying conflicts between the two nations. Analysts suggest that while the market response has been positive, the long-term implications of the ceasefire remain uncertain. The agreement is seen as a temporary measure, with experts calling for more comprehensive negotiations to address the broader geopolitical challenges. The situation continues to evolve, with stakeholders closely monitoring developments.
